name: Emergence
domain: Cellular automata, phase transitions, self-organization, complexity from simplicity
core_insight: Complex global behavior arises from simple local rules with no central controller. Near critical points, systems become scale-invariant: the same patterns appear at every scale. Self-organized criticality describes how many natural systems spontaneously tune themselves to the edge of a phase transition.

key_equations.name: Critical Exponent
key_equations.why: Order parameter vanishes at critical temperature with universal exponent β — same for many different systems

key_equations.name: Power Law
key_equations.why: Scale-free distributions: no characteristic size, heavy tails — signature of self-organized criticality

key_equations.name: Self-Organized Criticality
key_equations.why: Sandpile model: adding grains drives system to critical slope spontaneously, avalanches of all sizes

seed_prompt: ●● What are the local rules? Run them. What global pattern emerges that no local rule specified?
